title: Genetic aspects of non-polypoid colorectal neoplasms.
journal_full: Gastrointestinal endoscopy clinics of North America
abstract: Colorectal cancer is a heterogeneous disease arising through multiple possible pathways. Elucidating the genetic factors controlling molecular phenotype, morphology, histology, and prognosis of different tumor types continues to be a challenge. Non-polypoid colorectal neoplasms provide opportunities for ongoing study of their underlying genetic abnormalities and molecular phenotypes. The varied data from different groups, however, highlight the need for further studies in different populations.
